era: Seattle. Stoner Rock. E=6 -- thick riffs, fuzz, self-released, DIY underground.

name_references.monster_truck: Monster trucks: Bigfoot (Bob Chandler, 1975, St. Louis). Arena spectacle: oversized trucks crushing cars. Monster Jam. Distinctly American excessive entertainment.
name_references.thundercat: Thundercats: animated series 1985-1987, Rankin/Bass. Lion-O, Tygra, Cheetara, Panthro. Third Earth. 1980s animated icon.
name_references.note_thundercat_bruner: Thundercat = Stephen Bruner (bassist/vocalist, jazz/funk/soul collaborator with Kendrick Lamar and Flying Lotus) is a distinct entity from the animated series reference.
name_references.compound_significance: Stoner rock naming tradition: knowingly excessive, nostalgic, 1970s-80s pop culture referencing. Bongzilla, Earthride, Riffmaster General, Stoned Jesus, Wo Fat. Monster Truck Thundercat belongs to this tradition.

distribution.live: Small venue circuit: Tractor Tavern, Highline, Barboza, DIY spaces

double_stoner_degree.difference: 255: HPS international label. 256: self-released DIY. Same music, different institutional positions.
double_stoner_degree.significance: First consecutive stoner rock degrees in engine. Documents two simultaneous modes of existing as a stoner band.

arc_position.s36_side: Closes double stoner occurrence (255-256). E=6 holds.
arc_position.sps_side: OPENS STORM ARC (256-260). Winter Storm Surf E=8 ties SPS engine maximum.
arc_position.pairing_asymmetry: S36 E=6 vs SPS E=8. Deliberate: the storm is bigger than the music at this moment.

theory_nodes.concept: Stoner rock naming tradition: knowingly excessive 1970s-80s pop culture compounds. Monster Truck Thundercat operates in the tradition of Bongzilla, Earthride, Riffmaster General, Wo Fat. The name is the manifesto: deliberately excessive, nostalgic, deadpan humorous.
theory_nodes.description: Stoner rock naming as genre convention.

theory_nodes.concept: Self-released stoner rock in Bandcamp era: complete independence without marginalization. Bandcamp (2008) transformed the heavy psych underground. International reach without label infrastructure. Limited vinyl pressing circuit. Small venue touring without label booking. Monster Truck Thundercat demonstrate the viable self-released mode.
theory_nodes.description: Self-released stoner rock: Bandcamp distribution enables international reach.

theory_nodes.concept: Storm Arc opens (SPS 256). Winter Storm Surf E=8 ties SPS engine maximum. The outer coast of Washington in winter: massive Pacific swells. Storm arc projects to 256-260. The S36 side holds at E=6 while SPS opens at maximum energy. Asymmetric pairing: the storm is bigger than the music. The music continues anyway.
theory_nodes.description: Storm arc opens at degree 256. SPS E=8 maximum.

cross_references.relationship: Both Seattle stoner rock, E=6. First double stoner degree. Institutional contrast: HPS international vs. self-released DIY.
E=8 vs E=6. Storm arc opens. Asymmetric pairing: the ocean at maximum energy vs. the studio riff. The storm is bigger.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=6 vs E=8. STORM ARC OPENS. The outer coast of Washington in winter at E=8 ties SPS engine maximum. The stoner rock riff is heavy; the Pacific winter storm is categorically larger. The music holds its E=6 while the storm roars at maximum. Asymmetric pairing as intentional engine statement: the storm arc begins here.

milestones: SELF-RELEASED STONER DOCUMENTS COMPLETE DIY MODE
